message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
lets look at free and open source alternatives to middle (for 3 finger macos middle click)

attachment.content:
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Find open source macOS middle click alternative

input.description: Research FOSS alternatives to Middle app for macOS 3-finger middle click
input.prompt: Search the web for free and open source alternatives to the "Middle" macOS app (which enables 3-finger trackpad click to simulate middle mouse button). I want a list of FOSS options that work on macOS. For each, note: name, license, how it works (driver-level, accessibility API, etc.), GitHub/homepage URL, and any known limitations. Focus on solutions that are actively maintained or at least functional on recent macOS versions.

content.text:
Based on my research, here's a comprehensive list of free and open source alternatives to the Middle macOS app for enabling middle-click functionality:

## FOSS macOS Middle-Click Alternatives

### 1. **MiddleClick (artginzburg fork)**
- **License:** GPL-3.0
- **How it works:** Uses private macOS MultitouchSupport framework to intercept trackpad events and emulate middle-click on three-finger tap
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/artginzburg/MiddleClick
- **macOS Compatibility:** Sequoia 15 and later
- **Maintenance:** Actively maintained with regular updates (latest v3.2.0, 268 commits, 21 releases)
- **Language:** Swift (90.5%) + C
- **Installation:** Available via Homebrew: `brew install --cask middleclick`
- **Features:** System-wide middle-click, Safari integration, terminal paste support, customizable finger count (2-10 fingers), configurable tap duration and cursor tolerance
- **Limitations:** Requires Accessibility permissions; works only with built-in trackpad or Magic Trackpad

### 2. **MiddleClick (cl3m fork - Archived)**
- **License:** GPL-3.0
- **How it works:** Uses private macOS APIs for multitouch trackpad middle-click functionality
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/cl3m/MiddleClick
- **macOS Compatibility:** Up to macOS Mojave (10.14) - no longer maintained
- **Maintenance:** Archived since November 2021; maintainer recommends using artginzburg fork instead
- **Language:** Objective-C
- **Limitations:** Not recommended for modern macOS versions

### 3. **MiddleDrag**
- **License:** MIT
- **How it works:** Uses Apple's private MultitouchSupport framework to intercept raw touch data before system gesture processing
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/NullPointerDepressiveDisorder/MiddleDrag
- **macOS Compatibility:** macOS 15.0 (Sequoia) or later
- **Maintenance:** Actively maintained (206 stars, 40+ releases, active development)
- **Language:** Objective-C/Swift
- **Installation:** Homebrew (`brew install --cask middledrag`), MacPorts, or manual .pkg
- **Features:** Three-finger tap for middle-click, three-finger drag for middle-drag (useful for panning in CAD/design apps), coexists with Mission Control and other system gestures, menu bar configuration, launch-at-login
- **Limitations:** Requires macOS Sequoia 15.0+; requires Accessibility permissions

### 4. **MacPaste**
- **License:** Unlicense (public domain)
- **How it works:** Implements X11-style middle-click copy/paste by sending ⌘+C on selection release, positioning cursor on middle-click, then sending ⌘+V
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/lodestone/macpaste
- **macOS Compatibility:** Works on trackpads and Magic Mice via integration with third-party tools
- **Maintenance:** Unsupported/abandoned; maintainer notes original code disappeared and was replaced
- **Language:** C (88.7%) + Shell + Makefile
- **Installation:** Requires compilation via provided scripts
- **Features:** X11-style middle-click paste functionality across applications
- **Limitations:** Alters your clipboard; requires BetterTouchTool integration for trackpad support; unsupported and not recommended

### 5. **Mouser** (Limited Trackpad Support)
- **License:** MIT
- **How it works:** Lightweight mouse button remapper using driver-level access for Logitech mice; also supports keyboard/button remapping
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/TomBadash/Mouser
- **macOS Compatibility:** macOS 12 (Monterey) and later
- **Maintenance:** Actively maintained (multiple recent releases)
- **Language:** Swift/Objective-C
- **Features:** Button remapping, DPI control (200-8000), per-app profiles, 40+ built-in actions, gesture detection
- **Limitations:** Primarily designed for Logitech HID++ mice, not trackpad-focused; limited direct middle-click support through trackpad gestures

---

## Summary Table

| Tool | License | Implementation | Maintenance | macOS Compat | Recommended |
|------|---------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|-------------|
| MiddleClick (artginzburg) | GPL-3.0 | Private MultitouchSupport API | Active | Sequoia 15+ | ✅ Yes |
| MiddleDrag | MIT | Private MultitouchSupport API | Active | Sequoia 15+ | ✅ Yes |
| MacPaste | Unlicense | Clipboard automation | Abandoned | All | ❌ No |
| MiddleClick (cl3m) | GPL-3.0 | Private APIs | Archived (2021) | Up to Mojave | ❌ No |
| Mouser | MIT | Driver-level (mice-focused) | Active | Monterey+ | ⚠️ Limited |

---

## Recommendations

**Best overall choice:** **MiddleClick (artginzburg)** - GPL-3.0, actively maintained, works with Sequoia 15+, available via Homebrew, and offers the most configuration options.

**Best for drag operations:** **MiddleDrag** - MIT licensed, newer actively-maintained alternative that adds three-finger drag support, useful for CAD and design applications.

**Note:** Both primary options require private macOS APIs and Accessibility permissions but are legitimate, widely-used solutions without known security issues.

Sources:
- [artginzburg/MiddleClick on GitHub](https://github.com/artginzburg/MiddleClick)
- [cl3m/MiddleClick on GitHub](https://github.com/cl3m/MiddleClick)
- [NullPointerDepressiveDisorder/MiddleDrag on GitHub](https://github.com/NullPointerDepressiveDisorder/MiddleDrag)
- [lodestone/macpaste on GitHub](https://github.com/lodestone/macpaste)
- [TomBadash/Mouser on GitHub](https://github.com/TomBadash/Mouser)
- [Middle-click on macOS, free and open-source by Prerak Mann](https://mannprerak2.medium.com/middle-click-on-macos-free-and-open-source-ea68bc019ab7)
- [11 MacOS apps to increase productivity with trackpad - DEV Community](https://dev.to/ris58h/11-macos-apps-to-increase-productivity-with-trackpad-1co0)

- **License:** MIT
- **How it works:** Uses Apple's private MultitouchSupport framework to intercept raw touch data before system gesture processing
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/NullPointerDepressiveDisorder/MiddleDrag
- **macOS Compatibility:** macOS 15.0 (Sequoia) or later
- **Maintenance:** Actively maintained (206 stars, 40+ releases, active development)
- **Language:** Objective-C/Swift
- **Installation:** Homebrew (`brew install --cask middledrag`), MacPorts, or manual .pkg
- **Features:** Three-finger tap for middle-click, three-finger drag for middle-drag (useful for panning in CAD/design apps), coexists with Mission Control and other system gestures, menu bar configuration, launch-at-login
- **Limitations:** Requires macOS Sequoia 15.0+; requires Accessibility permissions

### 4. **MacPaste**
- **License:** Unlicense (public domain)
- **How it works:** Implements X11-style middle-click copy/paste by sending ⌘+C on selection release, positioning cursor on middle-click, then sending ⌘+V
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/lodestone/macpaste
- **macOS Compatibility:** Works on trackpads and Magic Mice via integration with third-party tools
- **Maintenance:** Unsupported/abandoned; maintainer notes original code disappeared and was replaced
- **Language:** C (88.7%) + Shell + Makefile
- **Installation:** Requires compilation via provided scripts
- **Features:** X11-style middle-click paste functionality across applications
- **Limitations:** Alters your clipboard; requires BetterTouchTool integration for trackpad support; unsupported and not recommended

### 5. **Mouser** (Limited Trackpad Support)
- **License:** MIT
- **How it works:** Lightweight mouse button remapper using driver-level access for Logitech mice; also supports keyboard/button remapping
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/TomBadash/Mouser
- **macOS Compatibility:** macOS 12 (Monterey) and later
- **Maintenance:** Actively maintained (multiple recent releases)
- **Language:** Swift/Objective-C
- **Features:** Button remapping, DPI control (200-8000), per-app profiles, 40+ built-in actions, gesture detection
- **Limitations:** Primarily designed for Logitech HID++ mice, not trackpad-focused; limited direct middle-click support through trackpad gestures

---

## Summary Table

| Tool | License | Implementation | Maintenance | macOS Compat | Recommended |
|------|---------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|-------------|
| MiddleClick (artginzburg) | GPL-3.0 | Private MultitouchSupport API | Active | Sequoia 15+ | ✅ Yes |
| MiddleDrag | MIT | Private MultitouchSupport API | Active | Sequoia 15+ | ✅ Yes |
| MacPaste | Unlicense | Clipboard automation | Abandoned | All | ❌ No |
| MiddleClick (cl3m) | GPL-3.0 | Private APIs | Archived (2021) | Up to Mojave | ❌ No |
| Mouser | MIT | Driver-level (mice-focused) | Active | Monterey+ | ⚠️ Limited |

---

## Recommendations

**Best overall choice:** **MiddleClick (artginzburg)** - GPL-3.0, actively maintained, works with Sequoia 15+, available via Homebrew, and offers the most configuration options.

**Best for drag operations:** **MiddleDrag** - MIT licensed, newer actively-maintained alternative that adds three-finger drag support, useful for CAD and design applications.

**Note:** Both primary options require private macOS APIs and Accessibility permissions but are legitimate, widely-used solutions without known security issues.

Sources:
- [artginzburg/MiddleClick on GitHub](https://github.com/artginzburg/MiddleClick)
- [cl3m/MiddleClick on GitHub](https://github.com/cl3m/MiddleClick)
- [NullPointerDepressiveDisorder/MiddleDrag on GitHub](https://github.com/NullPointerDepressiveDisorder/MiddleDrag)
- [lodestone/macpaste on GitHub](https://github.com/lodestone/macpaste)
- [TomBadash/Mouser on GitHub](https://github.com/TomBadash/Mouser)
- [Middle-click on macOS, free and open-source by Prerak Mann](https://mannprerak2.medium.com/middle-click-on-macos-free-and-open-source-ea68bc019ab7)
- [11 MacOS apps to increase productivity with trackpad - DEV Community](https://dev.to/ris58h/11-macos-apps-to-increase-productivity-with-trackpad-1co0)

Two real options:

**MiddleClick (artginzburg)** — GPL-3.0, actively maintained, Homebrew-installable (`brew install --cask middleclick`). 3-finger tap triggers middle click. Sequoia 15+ required.

**MiddleDrag** — MIT, also active, adds 3-finger *drag* for middle-drag (useful in CAD/design tools). Same Sequoia 15+ requirement.

Both use Apple's private `MultitouchSupport` framework and need Accessibility permissions. No other meaningful FOSS options exist for modern macOS.

Which macOS version are you on?
